1: And to which of the saints wilt thou turn?
2: And envy slayeth the silly one.
3: But suddenly I cursed his habitation.
4: Neither is there is there any to deliver them them .
5: And the robber swalloweth up their substance.
6: Neither doth trouble spring out of the ground;
8: And unto God would I commit my cause:
10: And sendeth waters upon the fields:
11: That those which mourn may be exalted to safety.
12: So that their hands cannot perform their their enterprise.
13: And the counsel of the froward is carried headlong.
14: And grope in the noonday as in the night.
15: And from the hand of the mighty.
17: Therefore despise not thou the chastening of the Almighty:
18: He woundeth, and his hands make whole.
19: Yea, in seven there shall no evil touch thee.
20: And in war from the power of the sword.
21: Neither shalt thou be afraid of destruction when it cometh.
22: Neither shalt thou be afraid of the beasts of the earth.
23: And the beasts of the field shall be at peace with thee.
24: And thou shalt visit thy habitation, and shalt not sin.
25: And thine offspring as the grass of the earth.
26: Like as a shock of corn cometh in in his season.
27: Hear it, and know thou it it for thy good.
